title: carduus sanctae-balmae subsp. acicularis
intro: Carduus sanctae-balmae subsp. acicularis, a lesser-known subspecies of the Sacred Thistle plant, is a type of thistle that is native to the Mediterranean region and is characterized by its sharp, needle-like leaves and purple flowers. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues, such as indigestion and diarrhea, due to its anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties. The health benefits of Carduus sanctae-balmae subsp. acicularis include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and even exhibit antimicrobial properties, making it a valuable addition to herbal remedies. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and can be used to treat various health conditions, although it's essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using any herbal remedies.
